notes: File transfer and remote control software; Laplink Gold released 1997 added internet-based remote access; product line continues as Laplink PCmover and Laplink Everywhere (2022)
Client-to-LAN dial-up architecture; user treated as full network node; dominated corporate remote access 1992-1998; superseded by VPN over broadband
Dumb terminal model; computing at corporate LAN; only GUI/keystrokes traverse link; evolved into RDP (Microsoft) and ICA (Citrix)
Study describes as the dominant remote access bandwidth; superseded by 56k modems (1996-97), DSL/cable broadband (1999-2003)
Not mentioned in study but represents the successor architecture that displaced both remote node and remote control; IPSec VPN standardized 1998
Central hub in both remote access architectures described; still foundational
